rationale: HLA-A class I promoter shore; methylated in lymphoid vs open in myeloid (lineage generalization of the T_naive>Monocyte anchor, real subset Delta-beta approx 0.35)
HLA-B class I promoter shore; lymphoid methylated vs myeloid open, same direction as HLA-A
HLA-C class I promoter shore; lymphoid methylated vs myeloid open

HLA-DRB1 class II promoter shore; class II is OPEN/hypomethylated in antigen-presenting myeloid cells, so lymphoid (mostly T/NK) > myeloid methylation. CAVEAT: B cells (lymphoid) also express class II and dilute the lymphoid mean - direction corrected from the subset panel which had it backwards (e<1)
HLA-DQB1 class II promoter shore; same APC-hypomethylation logic as DRB1 (lymphoid > myeloid), same B-cell dilution caveat
L1HS young LINE-1 5'UTR/promoter (rmsk chr2:160114516-160120680 +strand); young L1 hypomethylated/derepressed in myeloid vs lymphoid, so lymphoid > myeloid
L1HS young LINE-1 5' end (rmsk chr9:2515633-2521964 -strand); myeloid-hypomethylation hypothesis, lymphoid > myeloid, independent locus from chr2
HERV-K(HML-2) 5' LTR5_Hs (rmsk full-length provirus chr5:30486652-30496098 -strand); HERV-K LTR derepression in myeloid, lymphoid > myeloid
HERV-K(HML-2) 5' LTR5_Hs (rmsk full-length provirus chr12:58327458-58336915 -strand); re-included from the subset panel where it was coverage-pruned at n=3/group - lineage n=30/17 should power it
